  • Okavango Delta

Botswana • Southern Africa

Okavango Delta

One of Africa’s most exclusive safari ecosystems with mokoro channels and floodplain camps.

★ 4.9 341 reviews
Image slot Attraction Okavango Delta
Image slot Country Botswana
Image slot Region Southern Africa

About this attraction

The Delta is best for travelers who value wilderness intimacy, guiding quality, and a low-density safari atmosphere.

How to get there

Most itineraries route through Maun and continue by charter or light aircraft into camps.

Best time to visit

July to October is the classic flood-and-dry-season viewing period, though timing varies by concession.

Highlights

  • Independent landing page with a clear planning summary
  • Nearby accommodation and dining recommendations
  • External booking CTA connected to a partner page
  • Practical access and timing guidance before booking

Practical information

Use this page to understand arrival logistics, time commitment, where to stay nearby, and which local tour operators are best placed to support the experience.

Full description

The Delta is best for travelers who value wilderness intimacy, guiding quality, and a low-density safari atmosphere. Trek Africa Guide positions this as a practical planning page, so travelers can compare route logic, nearby stays, dining, and partner booking options before leaving the site.

Tour operators active here

Botswana Journey Studio

A locally grounded tour operator active in Botswana, helping travelers combine attractions, stays, transport, and realistic route timing rather than selling disconnected components.

Nearby stays

Accommodations near Okavango Delta

Image slot Stay Camp Okavango

Delta safari camp

Camp Okavango

A water-and-wilderness camp for travelers prioritizing mokoro channels, guided walks, and low-density Delta safari.

★ 4.8 (164) From $900 / night
Nxaragha Island, Okavango Delta
View Details

Nearby dining

Restaurants near Okavango Delta

Image slot Restaurant Camp Okavango Dining

Delta camp dining

Camp Okavango Dining

Okavango dining is normally included in the camp stay because there are no casual standalone restaurant circuits inside the Delta.

★ 4.5 (91) $$$$
Camp meals, high tea, and safari-day dining
